result.markdown:
--- title: "Traffic Stops with SQL" author: "Josh Goldhaber" format: html: code-fold: true echo: true warning: false message: false fig-cap-location: bottom editor: visual --- # Introduction In this project I use three policing datasets from the Stanford Open Policing Project (Pierson et al. 2020): New Orleans Police Department (LA), Madison Police Department (WI), and Louisville Metro Police Department (KY). All data wrangling is conducted entirely in SQL. I focus on two central questions: (1) how search rates vary across race and gender, and (2) whether hit rates (the percent of searches that find contraband) differ across agencies. The goal is to compare policing practices across these jurisdictions and identify meaningful differences in how traffic stops escalate into searches and how often searches actually find contraband. I first explore the structure of the database to understand what tables are available and how they are named. I then build a combined dataset that stacks three agencies into a single table and adds consistent indicators for searches and contraband. Finally, I summarize search and hit rates by race and gender and visualize patterns across cities. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} library(tidyverse) library(DBI) library(ggplot2) library(dplyr) con_traffic <- DBI::dbConnect( RMariaDB::MariaDB(), dbname = "traffic", host = Sys.getenv("TRAFFIC_HOST"), user = Sys.getenv("TRAFFIC_USER"), password = Sys.getenv("TRAFFIC_PWD") ) ``` ::: ## Inspecting the Database Structure ::: {.cell} ```{.sql .cell-code} SELECT table_name, SUBSTRING_INDEX(table_name, '_', 1) AS state FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema = DATABASE(); ```
Table: Displaying records 1 - 10 |table_name |state | |:----------------------------|:-----| |ar_little_rock_2020_04_01 |ar | |az_gilbert_2020_04_01 |az | |az_mesa_2023_01_26 |az | |az_statewide_2020_04_01 |az | |ca_anaheim_2020_04_01 |ca | |ca_bakersfield_2020_04_01 |ca | |ca_long_beach_2020_04_01 |ca | |ca_los_angeles_2020_04_01 |ca | |ca_oakland_2020_04_01 |ca | |ca_san_bernardino_2020_04_01 |ca |
::: # Build Three-City Dataset (SQL written once) ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} query_three_city <- " SELECT * FROM ( -- New Orleans (recode outcome) SELECT 'New Orleans' AS city, subject_race, subject_sex, date, time, CASE WHEN outcome = 'citation' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS citation_issued, CASE WHEN outcome = 'warning' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS warning_issued FROM la_new_orleans_2020_04_01 WHERE subject_race IS NOT NULL UNION ALL -- Madison SELECT 'Madison' AS city, subject_race, subject_sex, date, time, citation_issued, warning_issued FROM wi_madison_2023_01_26 WHERE subject_race IS NOT NULL UNION ALL -- Louisville SELECT 'Louisville' AS city, subject_race, subject_sex, date, time, citation_issued, warning_issued FROM ky_louisville_2023_01_26 WHERE subject_race IS NOT NULL ) AS t " ``` ::: ### Load the cleaned data into R ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} three_city <- dbGetQuery(con_traffic, query_three_city) ``` ::: ### Summaries ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} race_city_summary <- three_city |> group_by(city, subject_race) |> summarise( stops = n(), citation_rate = mean(citation_issued), warning_rate = mean(warning_issued), .groups = "drop" ) race_city_summary ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` # A tibble: 17 × 5 city subject_race stops citation_rate warning_rate 1 Louisville asian/pacific islander 1621 0.829 0.171 2 Louisville black 45947 0.715 0.285 3 Louisville hispanic 6656 0.757 0.243 4 Louisville other 781 0.201 0.799 5 Louisville unknown 2 1 0 6 Louisville white 91470 0.804 0.196 7 Madison asian/pacific islander 14957 0.692 0.308 8 Madison black 68638 0.696 0.304 9 Madison hispanic 28344 0.748 0.252 10 Madison other 1868 0.735 0.265 11 Madison white 213925 0.701 0.299 12 New Orleans asian/pacific islander 3793 0.376 0.288 13 New Orleans black 349819 0.211 0.239 14 New Orleans hispanic 13491 0.340 0.222 15 New Orleans other 342 0.415 0.251 16 New Orleans unknown 3214 0.423 0.318 17 New Orleans white 129703 0.266 0.261 ``` ::: ::: ### Visualization ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} ggplot(race_city_summary, aes(x = subject_race, y = citation_rate, fill = city)) + geom_col(position = "dodge") + labs( title = "Citation Rates by Race Across Three Cities", x = "Race", y = "Citation Rate (Proportion of Stops)", fill = "City" ) + theme_minimal() + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](SQL_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-6-1.png){width=672} ::: :::
